# Overview

    :stub-columns: 1

    * - docs

% end-badges

zfit model fitting interface for HEP

- Free software: BSD 3-Clause License

## Installation

```
pip install zfit-interface
```

You can also install the in-development version with:

```
pip install https://github.com/zfit/zfit-interface/archive/master.zip
```

## Documentation

<https://zfit-interface.readthedocs.io/>

## Development

To run the all tests run:

```
tox
```

Note, to combine the coverage data from all the tox environments run:

```
.. list-table::
    :widths: 10 90
    :stub-columns: 1

    - - Windows
      - ::

            set PYTEST_ADDOPTS=--cov-append
            tox

    - - Other
      - ::

            PYTEST_ADDOPTS=--cov-append tox

```

methods.name: zfit_interface.func.ZfitScalarFunc.values
documentation: Values of a histogram, corresponds to the integral over *func* for the bin edges.

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.param.ZfitParameter.set_value
documentation:
Set the {py:class}`~zfit.Parameter` to `value` (temporarily if used in a context manager).

This operation won't, compared to the assign, return the read value but an object that *can* act as a context
manager.

Args:

: value: The value the parameter will take on.

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.param.ZfitParameter.step_size
documentation:
Step size of the parameter, the estimated order of magnitude of the uncertainty.

This can be crucial to tune for the minimization. A too large `step_size` can produce NaNs, a too small won't
converge.

Returns:
    The step size
"""If the parameter has limits set or not."""

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.model.ZfitModel.integrate
documentation:
Integrate the function over `limits` (normalized over `norm_range` if not False).

Args:
    limits: the limits to integrate over
    norm_range: the limits to normalize over or False to integrate the
        unnormalized probability
    name:

Returns:
    The integral value

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.model.ZfitModel.register_analytic_integral
documentation:
Register an analytic integral with the class.

Args:
    func:
    limits: |limits_arg_descr|
    priority:
    supports_multiple_limits:
    supports_norm_range:

Returns:

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.model.ZfitModel.partial_integrate
documentation:
Partially integrate the function over the `limits` and evaluate it at `x`.

Dimension of `limits` and `x` have to add up to the full dimension and be therefore equal
to the dimensions of `norm_range` (if not False)

Args:
    x: The value at which the partially integrated function will be evaluated
    limits: the limits to integrate over. Can contain only some axes
    norm_range: the limits to normalize over. Has to have all axes

Returns:
    The value of the partially integrated function evaluated at `x`.

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.model.ZfitModel.register_inverse_analytic_integral
zfit_interface.old.model.ZfitModel.sample
documentation:
Sample `n` points within `limits` from the model.

Args:
    n: The number of samples to be generated
    limits: In which region to sample in
    name:

Returns:
    Tensor(n_obs, n_samples)

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.result.ZfitResult.hesse
documentation:
Calculate for `params` the symmetric error using the Hessian matrix.

Args:
    params: The parameters  to calculate the
        Hessian symmetric error. If None, use all parameters.
    method: the method to calculate the hessian. Can be {'minuit'} or a callable.

Returns:
    Result of the hessian (symmetric) error as dict with each parameter holding
        the error dict {'error': sym_error}.

        So given param_a (from zfit.Parameter(.))
        `error_a = result.hesse(params=param_a)[param_a]['error']`
        error_a is the hessian error.

methods.name: zfit_interface.old.result.ZfitResult.errors
documentation:
Calculate and set for `params` the asymmetric error using the set error method.

Args:
    params: The parameters or their names to calculate the
         errors. If `params` is `None`, use all *floating* parameters.
    method: The method to use to calculate the errors. Valid choices are
        {'minuit_minos'} or a Callable.

Returns:
    A `OrderedDict` containing as keys the parameter names and as value a `dict` which
        contains (next to probably more things) two keys 'lower' and 'upper',
        holding the calculated errors.
        Example: result['par1']['upper'] -> the asymmetric upper error of 'par1'

methods.name: zfit_interface.pdf.ZfitPDF.pdf
documentation: Probability density of the defined function, normalized over *norm* to 1.

methods.name: zfit_interface.pdf.ZfitPDF.ext_pdf
documentation:
Probability density of the defined function, normalized over *norm* to the yield.

This method is only available for extended PDFs.

methods.name: zfit_interface.pdf.ZfitPDF.counts
documentation:
Counts of a histogram, corresponds to the integral over *ext_pdf* for the bin edges.

This method corresponds to the "frequency" of a histogram.

This method is only available for extended PDFs.

methods.name: zfit_interface.pdf.ZfitPDF.rel_counts
documentation:
Relative counts of a histogram, corresponds to the integral over *pdf* for the bin edges.

This method corresponds to the "relative frequency" of a histogram.

methods.name: zfit_interface.variables.ZfitBinning.__getitem__
documentation: Get the pair of edges (not discrete) or bin label (discrete).

methods.name: zfit_interface.variables.ZfitBinning.__len__
documentation: Return the number of bins (not counting flow bins, which are ignored for this Protocol currently).

methods.name: zfit_interface.variables.ZfitBinning.__iter__
documentation: Useful element of a Sequence to include.

methods.name: zfit_interface.variables.PlottableTraits.discrete
documentation: True if each bin is discrete - Integer, Boolean, or Category, for example

classes.name: zfit_interface.variables.ZfitParam
documentation:
A parameter is primarily used to communicate intentions in two cases:

1. for a minimizer. It tells the minimizer what the initial value is, what the limits are
and the stepsize; also whether it is considered to be floating in the fit or fixed.
2. for a model. It is an unbinned variable that should be regarded, in the default case,
as
